sessions.intent: Design the filesystem permission system: signed per-app grants on paths, with read/write/delete verbs each carrying sub-options. Path-shaped rewrite of IPLangFileSystem.

before.assumptions: Current FileAccessControl is runtime-only (in-memory FileAccessControl record, populated by yes/no/always prompt at exception time). No signature, no expiry, no audit. IPLangFileSystem inherits System.IO.Abstractions.IFileSystem (string-only). Cross-app reads (e.g. a messages app reading every app's system.sqlite) are not modelable today.
before.risk: Big surface rewrite — every action that touches the FS and every runtime internal that does the same. Signature work has to walk the full call graph. Goal-mapped FS provider is deliberately deferred.

actions.context: Added new section 'OBP Variant Design — When a Concept Has Multiple Configurable Modes'. Codifies four rules drawn from this design pass: singular folders, variant records with default-allow, owners-do-their-own-coverage, methods take whole domain objects. Also added a refinement to existing OBP guidance: verb-named methods are fine when they do real work — the GetX/IsX smell is about property-shaped questions, not verbs in general.

v1 plan for filesystem permission system. Spine + four topic deep-dives + five stage files + summary. Design settled in conversation with Ingi; three open conversation gates documented before stage 3.

after.status: plan written; awaiting Ingi to settle the three open gates (app-side cascade, Content sub-option on Read, process-only grant distinction) before stage 3 starts. Stage 1 can begin in parallel — pure types are independent.
after.health: good — design walked end-to-end (file.read from Messages to Email/system.sqlite), every concept has a named owner, no choreography across files.
after.notes: Branch newly created from origin/runtime2. good_to_know.md edit is real codification work, not session noise — captures lessons that should outlast this branch.
